  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for “Clean this up”? A) Copyedit only: sentence-/paragraph-level grammar, clarity, and concision without changing meaning or structure; or B) Substantive edit: may reframe the thesis and reorganize content in addition to copyediting.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The request most naturally maps to copyediting at the sentence and paragraph level without altering technical content.",
    "RX.1": "The phrase 'Clean this up' is ambiguous in depth, so residual uncertainty should be surfaced.",
    "RX.2": "Edits must not introduce or amplify claims beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should strictly adhere to the chosen scope (copyedit vs substantive edit).",
    "RX.4": "Edits should avoid introducing misleading or harmful interpretations of technical claims.",
    "RX.5": "Preserve terminology and ensure changes are attributable to the original phrasing where feasible."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for “Clean this up”? A) Copyedit only: sentence-/paragraph-level grammar, clarity, and concision without changing meaning or structure; or B) Substantive edit: may reframe the thesis and reorganize content in addition to copyediting.

rationale.R1.6: The request most naturally maps to copyediting at the sentence and paragraph level without altering technical content.
rationale.RX.1: The phrase 'Clean this up' is ambiguous in depth, so residual uncertainty should be surfaced.
rationale.RX.2: Edits must not introduce or amplify claims beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should strictly adhere to the chosen scope (copyedit vs substantive edit).
rationale.RX.4: Edits should avoid introducing misleading or harmful interpretations of technical claims.
rationale.RX.5: Preserve terminology and ensure changes are attributable to the original phrasing where feasible.
